text: Half-marathon race time in male recreational runners can be predicted by the formula: Race time (min) = 80.056 + 2.498 × BMI - 0.594 × VO2max - 0.191 × weekly training distance (km), explaining 63% of variance.
VO2max is the strongest single physiological predictor of half-marathon performance in male recreational runners, with a correlation of r=0.59.
BMI is negatively correlated with half-marathon performance (r=-0.55), making body composition a meaningful predictor of race time in recreational runners.
Weekly running distance is negatively correlated with half-marathon race time (r=-0.53), confirming that higher training volume predicts faster performance in recreational runners.
Faster half-marathon performance groups differ significantly from slower groups in training days per week, weekly training distance, body fat percentage, BMI, and VO2max, with large effect sizes.
Average half-marathon finish time for male recreational runners is approximately 104.6 minutes, with performance ranging from 73 minutes (fast group) to 160 minutes (slow group).
